beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check plants
beats.reasoning: Eli starts the day at full energy on a sunny Monday. Morning routine includes tending to houseplants—a daily ritual—before heading out.

beats.activity: Commute to work via bus and walking
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm, so Eli takes the bus to Fremont instead. Leaves early to avoid the 11am FIFA match crowds heading downtown.
Morning shift at the clinic; Eli schedules around the 11am FIFA match by keeping clients moving and staying focused on work rather than the game.
Takes a long lunch break in the neighborhood. Sunny weather makes outdoor seating appealing. Skips the downtown FIFA bar scene—prefers staying local and low-key.
Afternoon shift wraps up before peak transit times. Light Rail closure ends at 3pm, but Eli finishes work anyway and avoids the rush.

beats.activity: Stop at local brewery supply shop for home brewing supplies
beats.reasoning: Detour on the way home to pick up malt and hops for a weekend brewing project. Sunny weather and good mood make it a perfect time to stock up.
Returns home with brewing supplies in hand. Light Rail is now operational again (closure ended at 3pm), but bus is fine for this route.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner and organize brewing setup
beats.reasoning: Cooks a simple dinner and lays out brewing gear for the weekend. Generous with money means quality ingredients. Time alone allows for reflection and planning.

beats.activity: Film photography work—develop negatives in darkroom; Wind down with tea and reading on the porch
beats.reasoning: Spends evening on a favorite hobby: developing film from the weekend's outdoor photography. Sunny day means good light captures to process.
Full 8 hours of sleep after a productive, low-stress day. Ends well before 6am cutoff on Tuesday.

diary:
Monday started like most do—plants got their water, coffee was strong. The clinic was steady; had a runner with shin splints and an older guy recovering from shoulder surgery. Both making real progress, which never gets old. Grabbed lunch at Molly Moon's, caught some folks watching the USA-Wales match on their phones. Didn't stick around for the full thing, but the energy downtown was buzzing even early in the day.

After work I swung by the homebrew supply shop in Ballard. Got some new hops and sanitizer—been meaning to dial in my process. Headed home as the afternoon light turned golden. Made a simple pasta dinner, then spent a few hours in the darkroom with some negatives I've been sitting on. There's something about that quiet red glow, watching an image emerge in the developer tray. Ended the day with tea on the porch, just reading and listening to the neighborhood wind down. Felt good. Felt settled.
thought_process: The World Cup match this morning almost pulled me in—I could feel the draw of it, everyone gathering at bars. But I had clients booked and honestly, I'm not that into soccer anyway. The light rail closure didn't affect me since I take the bus from Rainier Valley. My mood stayed pretty even throughout; work was satisfying, nothing stressful. I think the brewery supplies purchase was partly procrastination energy—I've been saying I'd upgrade my setup for weeks. By evening I was genuinely tired in a good way, so the darkroom felt perfect. The porch reading was the real reset, though. I needed that quiet after a day of being present for other people's bodies and movements.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
